# Summary

Describe the change in a few sentences.

# Motivation

Explain why this change is needed and what evidence supports it.

# Scope

## Included

- List what this Pull Request changes.

## Excluded

- List related work that is intentionally out of scope.

# Status and Evidence

State the current status using repository terminology and link or describe the evidence. Do not claim implementation without supporting repository artifacts.

# Validation

| Check | Result | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Not Applicable | Not Applicable | Explain why if no executable validation applies. |

# Security Considerations

Describe security impact, reviewed risks, and any sensitive information checks. Use `Not Applicable` with an explanation when appropriate.

# Cost Considerations

Describe cost impact. State explicitly when a change is documentation-only, local-only, or otherwise not cost-generating.

# Operational Considerations

Describe operational impact, deployment requirements, rollback concerns, or support considerations. Use `Not Applicable` with an explanation when appropriate.

# Documentation Changes

List documentation files changed and any links that reviewers should verify.

# Known Limitations

Document limitations, assumptions, or deferred work.

# Rollback or Recovery

Explain how to revert or recover from this change when applicable. Use `Not Applicable` with an explanation for documentation-only changes.

# Review Checklist

- [ ] Pull Request has one cohesive objective.
- [ ] Only intended files are changed.
- [ ] No secrets or sensitive information are included.
- [ ] No unsupported implementation claims are introduced.
- [ ] Documentation links are checked.
- [ ] Validation results are recorded, or `Not Applicable` is explained.
- [ ] Security impact is reviewed.
- [ ] Cost impact is reviewed.
- [ ] Required checks passed before merge.
